units.content: system identifies a first measurer and a second measurer, each represented as a target entity in storage
each measurer has one or more directed entries written against it by a further measurer
directed entries recording measurer attributes use the same six-field schema as entries recording physical target measurements
system computes a first attribute set for the first measurer from its directed entries, comprising property identifiers
system computes a second attribute set for the second measurer from its directed entries, comprising property identifiers
system computes symmetric difference between first and second attribute sets
system holds, for each target property of interest, a sensitivity set comprising property identifiers on which accuracy is dependent
system determines whether intersection of sensitivity set and symmetric difference is non-empty for each target property
system emits a machine-readable divergence-prediction signal identifying both measurers and the target property when intersection is non-empty
divergence-prediction signal is routed through the feedback-signal interface of claim 1 to the actor
analyser produces output entirely from directed entries in storage and sensitivity sets, with no other inputs
modification of an attribute-set entry for either measurer in storage causes corresponding modification of subsequent divergence-prediction outputs
analyser instructions remain unmodified when storage entries change, establishing data-driven adaptation without code change
